summary: Lesson plans generated for Week 6 teach Unit 2 content (Linear Equations) instead of Unit 1 content (Foundations: Numbers, Expressions). This is a direct result of executor.py not injecting the scope and calendar files, leaving the model working from goal description alone.

failures.criterion: Lesson plans reflect Unit 1 week, not new content
failures.expected: Week 6 should cover Foundations unit content: N-RN standards (number system) and A-SSE standards (expressions)
failures.actual: Week 6 covers Linear Equations in One Variable (8.EE.C.7) — this is Unit 2 content

failures.criterion: Standards cited are NY State grade 8-9
failures.expected: NY A-SSE.1a, A-SSE.1b (grade 8-9 expressions) or similar Unit 1 standards
failures.actual: 8.EE.C.7a and 8.EE.C.7b (which are real standards, but wrong unit). Also claims 8.EE.B.5-6 without context.
failures.note: Standards exist but are misapplied to wrong unit

failures.criterion: Assessment scoped to Unit content only
failures.expected: Unit 1 assessment: operations on numbers, expression simplification, understanding of mathematical language
failures.actual: Assessment covers multi-step equations and word problems (Unit 2 content)

failures.criterion: Warm-ups and exit tickets present and scoped correctly
failures.expected: Quick reviews of Unit 1 topics (numbers, expressions)
failures.actual: Warm-ups and exits review equations and problem-solving

root_cause.issue: executor.py does not load or inject scope and calendar files
root_cause.evidence: Task JSON references deliverables/scope_ALG1_fullyear.json and calendar as dependencies, but executor.py only passes task structure to model
root_cause.consequence: Model generates from goal description alone: 'Week 6 lesson plans for Algebra I' → infers 'end-of-unit review' → picks plausible unit (linear equations)
root_cause.test_reference: Test 1.2 in testing_plan.md identified this as 'a known gap'

what_is_working: ✓ Lesson plans ARE structured for 45-minute periods correctly
✓ Duration and timing breakdowns are realistic
✓ Warm-ups and exit tickets ARE present
✓ Teacher notes and pedagogical framing are thoughtful
✓ Differentiation strategies (station rotations, scaffolding) are appropriate
what_is_broken: ✗ Content does not match the scope file for this unit/week
✗ Standards are misapplied (right standards, wrong unit)
✗ No evidence the model read the actual scope or calendar

next_steps.action: Fix executor.py to inject scope and calendar as context before generating
next_steps.why: Without the actual scope, the model cannot align content to this course's structure
next_steps.complexity: Medium — requires loading JSON files and prepending to prompt

next_steps.why: Verify the model can generate correct Unit 1 content when given the actual scope

next_steps.action: Review Planner role spec for dependency handling
next_steps.why: Should clarify that scope/calendar are mandatory inputs, not optional

note_for_eric: The lesson plans are pedagogically sound — they would work well for a real Unit 2 Linear Equations week. But they're the wrong lesson plans for the wrong unit. This test caught exactly the gap your testing plan anticipated.
